content.body: We have recently moved to ShipBob and the transition has been incredibly smooth! This was definitely down to Lacey who guided us through every step and answered all of our countless queries. First impressions are that ShipBob is a well established company with great communication. So far, so good.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with their recent move to ShipBob and praising their onboarding experience and communication.

content.body: ShipBob customer support is always very helpful and very quick to respond. Would recommend them for your needs. The ShipBob platform is easy to use and navigate.

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipBob customer is praising their support responsiveness and platform usability.

content.body: Very pleased with shipbob so far! My company has been using shipbob for over a year now and we are excited to have them apart of our growth as we look to expand our company! Their site is very user friendly and takes the leg work out of fulfilling orders so you can put your focus in other aspects of your company! #teamshipbob

classification.summary: SHITI Coolers is a satisfied ShipBob customer of over a year looking to expand their company with their current fulfillment provider.

content.body:
Shipbob has been able to offer great solutions to our needs from a 3PL. We were nervous about the change but now we don't look back!
Installation of the App was extremely easy and we had someone help us onboard who was there whenever we needed, so it made the process far from intimidating. When it comes to speed and reliability of order ingestion, the process is fairly straight forward and easy. If you have inventory ready, the process is seamless. I would say the only hiccup is with Amazon FBA orders but Shipbob is always willing to help. Overall when it comes to tracking upload speed, communication of order progress, and speed ShipBob has been a great partner. As with any 3PL there will always be some issues, it's the nature of the business, but it's good to know that we can rely on ShipBob's technical support team to get things figured out.

classification.summary: Komuso Design is satisfied with ShipBob's 3PL services, praising their onboarding, speed, reliability, and support, with only minor issues around Amazon FBA integration.

content.body: I've had a great experience with ShipBob so far!It was really easy to install, quick and reliable order ingestion. Simple and effective tracking and I'm especially impressed with how the inventory syncs with my Shopify store and how easy it is to order new deliveries to my chosen warehouse.The support I received from my dedicated Implementation Manager, Sergio was outstanding as he made everything so simple and responded immediately to all of my queries. Thank you Sergio!I'm looking forward to growing my business internationally and I feel confident that ShipBob is the best fulfilment partner to help me achieve my business goals.

classification.summary: UK-based customer praising ShipBob's fulfillment services and planning to expand internationally.

content.body: Shipbob has been great to work with so far. I love that I have a dedicated account rep who can help me sort out whatever issue may be happening. I definitely am very happy with the service.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's service quality and dedicated account support, indicating high satisfaction with their current 3PL provider.

content.body: There are definitely pros and cons to shipbob. The shopify app works fine and they do have a lot of features, adding new tech features like EDI capability and integrations with SPSCommerce. However, on the b2b side, we have experienced issues. D2C works pretty seamlessly and connects to shopify with few issues.

classification.summary: Fera Pet Organics experiences issues with ShipBob's B2B fulfillment capabilities despite their D2C operations and Shopify integration working well.

content.body:
Couldn't be happier with ShipBob. Things they offer critical to our success are:
Ease of installationSpeed and reliability of order ingestionValue-add features like inventory sync, rules, and order processing delayTechnical support

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's installation ease, order processing speed, inventory features, and technical support.

content.body: We've got shipbob set up in 3 locations in the US, 1 in Canada, 1 in the EU, and 1 in the UK. It certainly reduces the amount of effort of managing multiple warehouse providers!

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipBob's multi-location fulfillment capabilities and appreciates the simplified management of multiple warehouses.

content.body:
Easy to use and integrates very well with Shopify.Very happy with it.
ShipBob has very responsive and helpful support for all our needs.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipBob's ease of use, Shopify integration, and responsive support.

content.body: Shipbob has made my fulfillment so much easier and efficient. My account manager, Neel B is incredible. He is always available, makes time to meet with me, and proactively shares methods for me to use to be more effective, efficient, and serve customers at a lower cost. I honestly view Neel as a member of my team and would feel lost without him. I appreciate this the most about Shipbob!

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipBob customer praising their account manager's proactive service and cost optimization support.

content.body: Overall ShipBob has been a good experience. My merchant success specialist has been a really big help. I do wish the app/website was more mobile friendly. It's very hard to navigate and many of the features are next to impossible to do when not on a computer. I work 90% from my phone so it's a struggle. The shopify integration is pretty solid. I do notice quite a bit of delay sometimes with tracking crossing over from shipbob to shopify. Sometimes it never crosses over at all so I have to add it manually but it's pretty rare. No problems with order syncing, it's fast and accurate. Overall I would recommend!

classification.summary: ShipBob user satisfied overall but frustrated with mobile app usability and occasional tracking sync delays with Shopify integration.

content.body: Soo glad we went with ShipBob. Their software was super easy to integrate with our Shopify store and our implementation specialist Lacey became an indispensable part of our team while onboarding, helping us through every question we could throw her way. The ability to have rule automations in place, inventory sync, and re-order points are excellent features to have and we are able to replenish Amazon, process and ship DTC orders, and fulfill case level wholesale skus. 5 Stars!

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's software integration, implementation support, and automation features for managing multi-channel fulfillment across DTC, Amazon, and wholesale.

content.body: We launched our first-ever e-commerce platform with Shopify and worked with ShipBob to manage all of our fulfillment.  Huge thanks to Lacey Elliott at ShipBob for making this process smooth and helping us whenever we needed it.   We would highly recommend ShipBob!- Ducks of a Feather marketing team

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment services and recommends them, showing no indication of switching providers.

content.body: Shipbob has been great to work with. Easy integration and thorough training and set up. They have helped us worth though any issues we had and have extremely responsive support.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's integration, training, support, and responsiveness as a fulfillment partner.

content.body: I am still new to the new ShipBob fulfillment experience, I am told things will pick up once I am more acclimated. So we are optimistic that is true

classification.summary: Customer is new to ShipBob fulfillment and optimistic about improving performance as they acclimate to the service.

content.body: The shipbob app is easy to use and the shopify integration is seamless. The realtime order sycing works well.

classification.summary: Wine Folly is satisfied with ShipBob's ease of use, Shopify integration, and real-time order syncing capabilities.

content.body: Scaling your business with a solid fulfillment partner can be a much easier task. ShipBob has reliable customer support and a willingness to solve issues and complex matters; it can be a bumpy ride initially, but the earlier you start the cooperation, the easier it will be when you scale. The easiness of managing shipments beyond your domestic market is also a big plus, and ShipBob covers most of the e-commerce needs. The integration with various services is also a plus. Overall, highly recommended.

classification.summary: Calidad Tools is satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment services and recommends them for scaling ecommerce businesses with reliable support and integrations.

content.body:
Using the this app has made it very easy for us to manage our fulfilment across a number of countries and not just in North Americas so it has saved a lot of time.
It was very easy to install as wasn't a private app and already in app store and the pulling down of orders can be set around the time frames we want which has been very import for when customer realise they have used old address or just typed it incorrectly.
The tracking is also passed back into Shopify from Shipbob as soon as delivery in transit which is sent on to our customer so saves us time again and our customers know what is happening with their delivery.
Technical support is also very quick to update us on courier issues as well as setting up import rules and tags when we see fraudulent orders...again this has saved us time and money on orders going bad.
Highly recommend the app and the company. :-)

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's fulfillment management app for its ease of use, multi-country support, order flexibility, tracking integration, fraud detection, and customer support.

content.body: Great service, the support is responsive and the shipping time is great. Tracking works well and their platform is easy to use.

classification.summary: Satisfied customer praising Saltbox's responsive support, fast shipping, reliable tracking, and user-friendly platform.

content.body: Shipbob is solid, it handles all of our fulfillment and it works as advertised. Easy to install, good functionality.

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipBob customer praising their fulfillment service's ease of use and functionality.

content.body: We are very happy with ShipBob FulFillment app and highly recommend ShipBob as a warehouse partner! The Fulfillment app is easy to install, very fast and reliable order ingestion and has many value-add features like inventory-sync, order processing delay, etc. Technical support is also a 10/10.

classification.summary: Customer is highly satisfied with ShipBob's fulfillment services and recommends them as a warehouse partner.

content.body: ShipBob is thorough with communication and great at assisting you in the setup process. It is easy to get set up and begin shipping! The merchant success specialists I have worked with have been great at answering questions and finding resolutions. There are some aspects we are not fond of, the costs of WROs and the precision required when sending supplies (we order from supply stores and have it sent directly there, so affixing labels is not always possible); they do not seem to work well with USPS (we have had 1 or 2 days in the last year where all of our orders from a specific day were labeled but never moved or it took a week or more for them to). All in all positive experience, I do recommend inquiring about fulfillment times if you are guaranteeing same-day shipping, etc.

classification.summary: Current ShipBob customer satisfied overall but frustrated with WRO costs, supply labeling requirements, and USPS fulfillment delays, recommending others verify fulfillment speed guarantees.

content.body: user friendly app, easy to install and the team walk you through the whole process during an onboarding call.Integrates seamlessly with our shopify store and makes fulfilling orders to customers a much more automated process.

classification.summary: Existing satisfied customer praising Saltbox's user-friendly app, seamless Shopify integration, and onboarding support that has improved their order fulfillment automation.

content.body:
App works fine, but the customer service and pricing is TERRIBLE.
We would CONSTANTLY be charged many times more because Shipbob insisted on sending our products in boxes that were much larger than our products. We're talking about nearly a $100 difference because they didn't want to use a smaller box to ship (which they have).
We are a small business, so seeing a shipping cost of $140 for something that should cost $30-$40 is terrible. Moreover, having to go back and forth with customer service to fix this issue causes delays and makes our customers extremely unhappy. This is not the first time and after customer service was contacted, they would apologize, issue a partial refund, and repeat the mistake. If any higher-ups see this, I will be happy to provide proof of correspondence so things can be changed.
Be very, very careful if you are thinking of using Shipbob services.
UPDATE:
I was contacted by Shipbob's VP or Partnerships and Director of Onboarding services soon after posting this review. They were sincere with their apology about my experience so far and it showed that they took this matter seriously. It doesn't make up for everything that transpired, but I will add two stars for trying to make things right and bringing immediate attention to my case.

classification.summary: Small business frustrated with ShipBob's oversized box usage causing excessive shipping costs and repeated billing errors despite customer service promises to fix the issue.

content.body:
ShipBob is easy to set up, orders are fulfilled promptly and by the most economical shipping carrier.
Leveraging their network of warehouses throughout the USA was straightforward and led to significant cost savings for us and reduced shipping times for our customers.
Their customer support is also excellent and there is always someone available to talk to if you have an issue.
Overall, ShipBob is an great option for small businesses looking to save on their shipping costs while reducing shipping transit times.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's warehouse network, fulfillment speed, cost savings, and customer support as a solution for small businesses.

content.body: Great integration, easy to set up for Shopify (and Amazon). We are a long-term ShipBob partner and recommend them as a 3pl service provider.

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipBob customer in the UK praising easy integration with Shopify and Amazon, recommending 3PL services.

content.body: Simple integration between ShipBob and Shopify, with solid online resources to help when in question. Great customer service at both ShipBob and Shopify to help iron out issues.

classification.summary: Moon Cheese is satisfied with ShipBob's integration with Shopify and their customer service support.

content.body: Love ShipBob Fulfillment, this is by far the best fulfillment company I found to easily fulfill products with Shopify and Amazon. Great customer service too. I recommend them.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's fulfillment services and Shopify integration, expressing satisfaction rather than dissatisfaction.

content.body:
Stay away, Shipmonk has been an headache for our business for the past year.We have been working with them for over 3 years.WE had to fulfilled some of our order after 3days SLA passed, they still charge for the minimum pick fee and never give you a credit for their mistake.They shipped wrong product to one of our distributors, with hand cream. it was so poor packed that everything was damaged and we were finned for wrong customs declarations.WE lost 10000 of dollars of products. second shipment destroyed by customs because of the first one.
We have been waiting for a wholesale order, it has been over 11 days, we have some presentation samples orders and we have to wait.
For the B2c Shipmonk has been taking over 3 days to fulfilled orders resulting in loss of customers and refunds. Yesterday one of my order was supposed to be fulfilled stil waiting......
When you complain, some of them are nice some others hung up on you or make you feel uncomfortable or send you a passive agressive email.
One thing they know it s to charge your credit card!

classification.summary: Customer is actively looking to leave ShipMonk due to repeated fulfillment delays, missed SLAs, poor customer service, incorrect shipments, and unexpected fees that have cost them over $10,000.

content.body: Great integration overall! I needed some assistance from support with both ShipBob and Shopify in order to get everything working, but I was also a bit of an edge use case in my needs. Once set up, it's been a breeze to use overall.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipBob's integration and ease of use after initial setup support, despite having complex requirements.

content.body: We've had a very easy time syncing orders and inventory with the ShipBob <> Shopify connector. The customer success and support team are knowledgeable and have quickly remedied any product issues.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipBob's Shopify integration and support team performance.

content.body: I moved to ShipBob after a clunky process with another fulfilment company, and I'm really happy I switched. The integration offers some great features and settings, and everything runs seamlessly. The set up was pretty straight forward too. Very happy with ShipBob!

classification.summary: Customer successfully switched from another fulfillment company to ShipBob and is satisfied with the integration and seamless operations.

content.body: I have a UK based business. Shipbob integrates easily with my shopify store. I was helped whenever I needed it to help me get up and running. Switched from Amazon FBA to Shipbob and very glad I did. Great app.

classification.summary: UK-based business successfully switched from Amazon FBA to ShipBob and is satisfied with the integration and customer support.

content.body: Our transition from Shopify to ShipBob Fulfillment has been nothing short of wonderful. The transition team, with Sarthak S. helping us as our designated Implementation Specialist, has been very professional, responsive, and helpful in answering our questions and providing solutions to issues that arose as we transitioned into using ShipBob. We truly appreciate all the help and guidance that we received, and we are very happy with our decision to move to ShipBob.

classification.summary: Customer is praising their successful transition to ShipBob Fulfillment and highlighting excellent implementation support.

content.body: Shipbob are great - Some of the best customer service I have had. Sergio, Sam and Enda are so supportive. I could not recommend this team anymore. It is also, slick tech and easy to useWe trust Shipbob because of the below:- Ease of installation- Speed and reliability of order ingestion- Tracking upload speed- Inventory sync, rules, and order processing- Technical support

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipBob's customer service, technology, and reliability across multiple operational areas.

content.body: The ShipBob team has been wonderful to work with. They are hands-on and fast to respond, resulting in a smooth integration!   Our rep, Samantha R. has given us the gold standard of customer service. She has repeatedly helped us with challenging orders & ensured the best customer care for our clients.  I highly recommend!

classification.summary: Thoughtfully.com is praising ShipBob's customer service and integration support, not expressing a need for Saltbox.

content.body: I am so frustrated at this app and the lack of support that we have been receiving. Ever since the update, Shipsation has been a total disappointment. We used it without any issues for the past 5 years but once the upgrade happened, nothing has been functioning properly. It's time-consuming to have to reach out to tech support constantly because their app isn't functioning properly. I am looking into a different fulfillment app.

classification.summary: Customer is actively seeking an alternative fulfillment solution after experiencing persistent technical issues and poor support from Shipsation following a recent app update.

content.body: RUN! RUN AS FAST AS YOU CAN! I really do feel bad for you if you have already signed a contract with shipbob, this is the worst fulfillment center you will ever find. Hopefully you can find a way out before it's too late. I will say the company was not always this bad. 2 years ago Shipbob was smaller and they actually did a ton to care for their customers and they took pride in doing good work. Come to present day and they have been sitting on a container of our product for 6 days now and still haven't even stored it let alone shipped. We are literally begging for an update and all they do is send us links to their SLA. We get it, however, our agent promised us this was priority and turns out that was NOT the case. I guess this is what happens when you put profits over customer experience. Plus, its not just us look at all of the most recent reviews. To make matter worse their shipping rates are extremely expensive and they constantly lose product. NO JOKE, this year they lost an entire skew of ours and we had to tell our most important customer than none of his 17 clubs would get the product. He was blown away that a center could be so careless and he laughed at us when we said we used shipbob. He called them the " NIGHTMARE OF FULFILLMENT CENTERS" lol In closing I am writing this to all of the entrepreneurs out there considering shipbob as a partner. You have worked your butt off to get where you are, poured your heart into your brand, don't make the mistake of going with a company that will destroy your brand image. Go find a partner that will help your hard work pay off.

classification.summary: Terra-Core Fitness is actively seeking to leave ShipBob due to severe service failures including delayed product storage, lost inventory, expensive shipping rates, and broken promises from their agent.

content.body: Love Ship station streamlines all my shipping into 1 central place.I am able to ship from multiple sales platforms and use my multiple courier accounts.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipStation's ability to centralize shipping from multiple sales platforms and courier accounts.

content.body: I have had this app for 1yr, so i did give them a good chance. Forget the app, there is much better out there loads. READ THE 1 STAR REVIEWS .ALL OF THEM,1 thing in common, they have the worst customer service, of any company i have dealt with, i had 3 small issues , so i was patient but this 3rd issue is costing me alot of money. now which is the only reason i a, writing this review. this is the 1st bad review i have wrote. Save yourself time and money. ITs not even worth having there free plan, customer support is too bad , really bad, no contact for days, then don't answer your question, then dont fix the issue, i have literally just got my other e-commerce shop owners, all 27 shops to all switch from this too SHIPPING IS EASY.. so much better app ,and customer support the best . Go read there reviews you will thank me

classification.summary: Customer switched 27 ecommerce shops away from this app due to poor customer service and unresolved issues, and now recommends Shipping Is Easy as a superior alternative.

content.body: We've used Shipstation for six years now. Their most recent update has completely ruined this software. Per support, we must uninstall and then reinstall their printing software each week (and have done so for the past several months). What's the point of shipping software that's unable to print labels?!

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user frustrated with recent software updates causing recurring printing failures that require weekly reinstallation workarounds.

content.body: their support technic team was really Helpful .But I was not really happy with the rates and it was quite complicated to choose a package/carrier. I did not enjoy my experience.

classification.summary: Customer appreciated support quality but was dissatisfied with pricing and complex package/carrier selection process, resulting in an overall negative experience.

content.body: The app doesn't put import/export data for international shipments: hts code, values, etc. It is causes problems, because they ignored it. It is in their api and simple to include. There is no reason to leave it out. It reduces the value of the the app and service.

classification.summary: User is frustrated that Saltbox's app lacks critical import/export data fields (HTS codes, values) for international shipments despite the capability existing in their API, reducing the app and service value.

content.body: I dont' like writing bad reviews but my experience with Easyship has been unbelievably bad. I launched my store, got my first order and what do you know? I can't ship anything. Apparently it's a problem at the back end but more than 24 hours later, I still don't have a resolution and have been told by customer service that I'm in a queue. I was also given a run down of the tech team's meeting on a Monday morning (supposedly so I'd know I wasn't first - or a priority). Put simply, I'm paying for a service that I cannot use. I'm in New Zealand by the way - so would definitely recommend that other NZ businesses look elsewhere.

classification.summary: Customer cannot use Easyship's service due to backend technical issues unresolved for 24+ hours and is actively seeking alternative shipping solutions.

content.body: I've been using the app for 4+ years. Used to be a great app but the recent updates turn it into total crap. The users on shared workstations can't print anymore . Tried to get assistance from support only to be told that "Because of the changes made to this version of ShipStation Connect, we recommend only having one user logged in at a single time " - hello, 1992 is speaking .

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user frustrated by recent updates that broke multi-user printing functionality and received dismissive support response.

content.body: I removed it after struggling soo much with the shipping.. It won't ship anything, anywhere and it's just bad business for small and new stores who try to grow up! Every time I open the app, it asks me to reconnect to my shop, I have to re-do the settings over and over again and it's for nothing... Waste of money and time!! Time is money! Save yourself!

classification.summary: User is abandoning their current shipping/fulfillment solution due to persistent technical issues, unreliable shipping functionality, and poor support for small/growing ecommerce stores.

content.body: Truly awful development team removing or breaking features with every update. Every bug report is ignored. Every feature request is ignored. Customer service team is useless, they will "forward over tickets to their dev team" and then respond after months and months of outage issue reports just to tell you they aren't doing anything. Based on my experience, I genuinely hope a good competitor puts them out of business.

classification.summary: Customer is extremely dissatisfied with poor development practices, ignored bug reports and feature requests, and unresponsive customer service, and is actively seeking an alternative provider.

content.body: Shipbob has put our amazon account in jeopardy with a 16% late ship rate. Technical support is a carousel that goes nowhere. They have an online "chat" window, where the representative does basically nothing but open a support ticket - which go unanswered. Additionally, they sent boxes of assembly parts to a customer (who had no idea why he got these miscellaneous plastic rings). This led to us having to product to assemble, costing us revenue. Most importantly, just google "Shipbob" and "BBB" and read the number of Better Business Bureau horror stories about Shipbob. Or just look at reviews and see how horrific the stories are. The onboarding is very slick, they put a lot of energy into getting you on, but they can't get products out, nor do they offer technical support that is useful or helpful.

classification.summary: ShipBob customer experiencing critical fulfillment failures including 16% late shipments, poor support, and order accuracy issues, actively seeking alternatives.

content.body: This was the best app we had. Once it was updated IT IS COMPLETE TRASH!! Their support will take weeks with no help. A glitch a day will make you feel like you work for Ship station and not your own business.

classification.summary: User is actively looking to switch from their current app due to poor post-update quality and inadequate support, feeling loss of control over their business operations.

content.body: Do not use shipstation if you plan on adding new products to your store on a regular basis. It seemed amazing until I found out that they require you to manually add information for every product you have such as weight, value, customs info and more...all info that was already added to shopify in the first place creating twice the amount of work. The workaround they gave me was to create a spreadsheet and re-import that every time I add a new product. Ridiculous.

classification.summary: User is frustrated with ShipStation's requirement to manually duplicate product information already in Shopify, creating inefficient workflows when scaling product catalog.

content.body: As a small business trying to expand globally, Easyship has been amazing at making international shipping headache-free. The personalised support we have gotten our account manager Krish has really simplified and enhanced our experience. We will definitely continue to use Easyship's services for years to come!

classification.summary: WildBoarGames is satisfied with Easyship's international shipping services and personalized account support, with no indication of switching providers.

content.body: I worked with several fulfilment centers in my career and never had a worse experience than with ShipBob. Our company kept receiving complaints from DTC customers that were receiving products heavily stained, dented and damaged, covered in black dust that looked like soot. We placed a number of trial orders and had the same issue every single time, products arriving in horrible conditions. After asking ShipBob multiple times for an explanation, product audit and resolution, we waited months and finally received appalling pictures that showed our expensive products "stored" in haphazard mounds on top of each other in filthy boxes on the floor. It was evident from the pictures that many units were stained, dented, broken, leaked. We requested a report of damaged vs salable units and a credit for the damages. After over three months, we still have received no answers on the counts and were told that all of our damages are due to a mysterious "manufacturing defect" and Ship Bob is refusing to cover for any of it. These are thousands upon thousands of dollars of loss plus DTC customers being lost due to the poor experience. Further to this, the customer service is absolutely useless for any matter beyond the simplest things and will just provide pointless "cut and paste" answers that do not resolve your issues. I absolutely DO NOT recommend this company to anyone who values their business and brand equity. I wish I could attach the horrible pictures for everyone to see.

classification.summary: Brand experienced severe product damage and storage issues with ShipBob resulting in thousands in losses and customer churn, with unresponsive customer service refusing accountability.

content.body: Very happy with the Shopify app.It makes shipping a super breeze.Really a good app.I highly recommend you to try them out

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with Shopify's shipping app functionality and recommends it to others.

content.body: EasyShip have been a fantastic partner for our brand. As a small startup, we had numerous areas we have to cover, many which were outside our wheelhouse. We found EasyShip and they helped us incorporate our fulfillment and order processing flow with ease. They offer great customer service and support, going above and beyond to help us get up to speed and ensure our logistics were covered. Thanks EasyShip!

classification.summary: A satisfied customer praising EasyShip's fulfillment services, customer support, and ability to handle their logistics needs as a startup.

content.body: If Zero starts was possible i will give it. Unless you are interested in ruining your business i will deeply suggest that you don't use shipbob. I read all the bad reviews online and still ignored it. Please don't be like me. Shipbob as completely ruined my business and left me with a chain of angry customers. For a whole month, all my international orders where shipped and automatically returned to sender. This is terrible because international shipping with shipbob costs a fortune. How can a business survive this. Let me explain how terrible this is. i spend $20 plus on ship an order that cost $60 including shipping fee. Shipbob ships the item unsuccessfully and then charge me $20 to return the item and now wants to charge me another $20 to reship the item. So basically i have lost $60 for every international order i got. That almost a $6000 dollar loss because this happened with close to 100 orders in one month. My terrible experience with shipbob has sent me into terrible depression. It has been the worst experience of my life. My paypal account and stripe is ruined because of multiple disputes due to lost packages and undelivered items. Please do not use shipbob, they will ruin your life and the business you've worked so hard to build.

classification.summary: Customer experienced catastrophic failures with ShipBob's international shipping resulting in ~$6000 in losses and is actively warning others against using them while seeking an alternative fulfillment provider.

content.body: I love how easy it is to use Shipstation with Shopify! I get an order on shopify and it integrates very easily. I would recommend shipstation for all of you selling channels.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipStation's easy integration with Shopify and recommending it to other sellers.

content.body: ShipStation is the hub of my business. It makes my life so simple. I've connected my shopify store as well as all the other marketplaces where I sell. After several years of use, Shipstation continues to import all my order flawlessly.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipStation's reliability and integration capabilities for their multi-channel ecommerce business.

content.body: Getting customer support to answer questions is like pulling teeth. I've had a few occasions where Shipstation wouldn't print, I would make labels in bulk and then couldn't print, and assorted other issues. They take over a week to answer meanwhile you're trying to run a business. Annoying.

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with slow customer support response times and technical issues with ShipStation's printing functionality that are impacting their business operations.

content.body: Very intuitive and easy to use! The tech support team is also responsive within 24 hours. I'd recommend!

classification.summary: War Locker is satisfied with an intuitive platform and responsive tech support, indicating positive experience with a fulfillment or logistics service.

content.body: I'm hoping it gets better but looking bad so far. My first order sat for four days before they shipped it out. One of things most advertised about ShipBob for Shopify store owners is their direct integration with the stores. That does not seem to include tracking notifications to the customer, so now instead of fulfilling orders, I'm constantly checking my phone waiting for confirmation that an order was eventually shipped so I can send to my customer. Got a bad feeling about this.

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with ShipBob's slow order fulfillment and lack of automated tracking notifications, forcing them to manually monitor shipments instead of having reliable fulfillment operations.

content.body: I use this app to help fulfill our orders efficiently. It has worked great in the 3+ months that I have been working where I currently am. So far I have not had any real issues with Easyship.

classification.summary: Satisfied customer praising their current fulfillment app (Easyship) for reliable performance over 3+ months with no issues.

content.body: I have used shipstation for years for many of my clients. very good streamlining system. I recommend!

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipStation user is recommending the platform to others based on years of positive experience.

content.body: DO NOT go with Shipbob. Several hidden fees that they were not transparent about before coming onboard. If you're an apparel brand or any brand with several SKUs (i.e. size/color combinations), their warehousing costs are astronomical. They are 6X the price of my previous warehouse and cost more than rent for an entire house in the area. I'm moving my inventory ASAP and deeply regret the headache and money its costs me moving to Shipbob.

classification.summary: Apparel brand actively moving inventory away from ShipBob due to non-transparent hidden fees and warehousing costs 6X higher than previous provider, particularly problematic for multi-SKU operations.

content.body: Great app for small businesses and includes everything you need to ship hundreds of orders a week with little-to-no hassle. Built-in UPS rate is also absolutely fantastic through them Would highly recommend.

classification.summary: Satisfied customer praising an app/service for handling high order volumes efficiently with good shipping rates.

content.body: This app is super helpful in making shipping with our Shopify orders. Manually enter customer information when products are ready to ship is a thing of the past. Very helpful.

classification.summary: Element Glo is satisfied with a shipping app that automates manual customer information entry for Shopify orders, reducing fulfillment friction.

content.body: We use Shipstation every day for our E-commerce store. Makes shipping quick and easy. The support is great, Stancey always takes care of us

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipStation's shipping capabilities and support quality, not indicating any need to switch services.

content.body: We have used shipstation for quite some time and it has been great. We have connected multiple store types and it has run through them with ease. It is very intuitive and easy to use. It is a breeze to work with.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipStation's ease of use and multi-store integration capabilities.

content.body: We've been using Easyship for almost 2 years - it's been a game changer for optimizing our postage spend. The integration with Shopify works really well, and the customer service is excellent. Our CSM is super responsive, and always willing to help with any issues that come up.

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with their current 3PL solution (Easyship) for postage optimization and Shopify integration with strong customer service support.

content.body: Loyal ShipStation user for years, had gotten quite used to things and everything ran fairly smoothly. Had gotten the new layout forced upon me but always could switch back. Now that the V3 is rolled out and forced upon us with no reverting, our ability to ship effortlessly is impossible. We have been on support calls for hours only to be told "huh, that's weird" and no solution in sight. Now having to manually adjust every one of our hundreds of orders because of glitches and bugs in this new layout that they have been working on for over a year. It's really sad, I loved this stuff, and now it's borderline unusable, and searching for other solutions.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user is actively seeking alternative solutions due to critical bugs and poor support in the forced V3 update that is making order fulfillment unmanageable.

content.body: ShipHero to the rescue. The app has a great UI, which makes it super easy to train our employees to use the new system. Our onboarding specialist was super helpful and a blast to work with. I will say it does seem like sometimes they will push new updates and sometimes those updates can cause the app to act a little buggy. Their support is good and they normally will have a fix or solution that day. Definitly I would say for small to medium size businesses this app is awesome.

classification.summary: YoungLA is satisfied with ShipHero's user-friendly interface and support, though they occasionally experience bugs after updates, and recommends it for small to medium businesses.

content.body: On the Inventory distribution side of things, Shipmonk has made it really easy to send inventory and determine its levels. Its' platform if very user friendly as it provides useful metrics such as stockout days and low inventory levels. The happiness engineers are awesome at providing help and clarification on any of our company's requests.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipMonk's inventory management platform, user-friendly interface, and customer support quality.

content.body: I am very pleased with shipstation. ShipStation is easy to use and the customer service is amazing. I would recommend ShipStation to any business.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipStation's ease of use and customer service with a 5-star review, not expressing any need for alternative fulfillment solutions.

content.body: ShipStation recently forced users into a new interface that is awful and buggy. It now takes much longer to get packages out the door and creating returns and return labels takes twice as long as it used to. Good luck getting anyone from ShipStation to respond to help requests. Actively looking for a replacement because of how slow it's made fulfilling orders.

classification.summary: DinkyDogClub is actively seeking a fulfillment solution to replace ShipStation due to a slow, buggy interface that has significantly increased order processing and returns handling time.

content.body: I've used this app for literal years and years, and it's been perfectly fine. Ever since they updated the layout and forced it on us with no option to revert it's been buggy, glitching out at anything more than a few orders at a time, and have completely disrupted the work flow many of us have been using for years. Now it's taking 2-3x as long to do the same basic stuff we used to do. Please use Pirate Ship or something else. Ship Station sucks

classification.summary: Long-time user frustrated with recent app updates causing performance degradation and workflow disruption, actively seeking alternative shipping solutions.

content.body: I've been using Shipstation for about 5 years now, and this most recent update is absolutely horrible. They made it very hard to split orders, and the newly split orders no longer have their own order number. Now, as of July 1st, you can't even split orders anymore. I'm sure it's just a glitch, but when we have a large order that needs to be split into multiple shipments, we can't do it anymore. Printing labels now takes extra unnecessary steps. When you hover over an order, order details automatically pop up in a screen in front of you and doesn't go away until you click the popup away. That's annoying. They also took my custom layout for printing and wiped it clean with this new update, so I have to re-establish that. This new update is absolutely horrible, and good luck figuring out how to contact them. I am on the Gold plan and it took me 30 minutes to figure out how to contact them because help just says that if I'm on the gold plan, I should click the ? and a chat button will be there. There is no chat button. I actually have no idea what I clicked on in 30 minutes of searching to make the chat button appear, but it was ridiculous. This update will more than likely cause us to leave Shipstation after many years with them because each day it just gets worse.

classification.summary: Long-time Shipstation user is extremely frustrated with recent updates that removed split order functionality, complicated workflows, and poor customer support, and is actively planning to leave for a different solution.

content.body: So many glitches. We have lost over $1000 in orders going incorrectly to the wrong people due to label glitches and another glitch where it would not mark an order sent so our team sent it twice. Definitely do not recommend.

classification.summary: Customer experienced significant financial losses ($1000+) due to fulfillment errors including mislabeled orders and duplicate shipments, resulting in dissatisfaction with the service.

content.body: When it is working fine, it is a treat to use. Integration with our Shopify store is a must, and it usually work great. Lots of features and integrations with carriers. Structurally, I have little to complain about. However, the app is glitchy sometimes. Various pieces stop working for short periods of time, although their "uptime meter" claims everything is A-OK. More importantly, however, their service has been getting sloooooooow. I've had 2 tickets open for days, and nobody has helped me yet. One of the tickets is essential to our business to be tended to immediately, yet... crickets. The service was always a bit lacking, but by this point they badly need more support staff.

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with slow service, unresponsive support tickets, and occasional glitches despite generally positive features and Shopify integration.

content.body: Shipping orders themselves seem to be going alright, but I've been dumbfounded at the number of basic things we've asked for where the response has been "You're the first person to ever ask about this feature!" which I have a hard time believing. For example, printing one Order Summary per page when bulk printing is not an option (seriously?). The most recent shock has been that, in order to simply increase the font size of the packing slips/order summaries so our more elderly staff members can read them, I have to upgrade to a higher plan that unlocks "customization" of documents. I don't want to fully customize the documents, I just want the font bigger so our staff can read it. Sucks that they want to charge us an extra $240 per year for the privilege of printing readable documents. We're in too deep now to switch apps, but my advice to anyone considering this would be to ask all those "seemingly dumb" questions you have about features so you're not shocked about what's not included down the road.

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with hidden feature limitations and forced plan upgrades for basic functionality like font size adjustments on packing slips, but feels locked into their current solution.

content.body: We've been with ShipMonk for awhile now and they have been absolutely wonderful! When we first partnered with them they were a bit smaller and still somewhat new, since then they've grown into a larger 3PL company, with updated backend apps/software, more staff, more features, warehouses, better prices, etc. The backend software/dashboard is intuitive, constantly getting updated, it shows your whole business at a glance and is easy to see and edit your orders from a birds-eye-view. After the initial onboarding process, everything integrates perfectly with Shopify. We've personally had little to no problems with ShipMonk themselves, errors are usually caused by USPS or other shipping companies. When we do have an issue or concern, the "Happiness Engineers" at ShipMonk are phenomenal, they're your personal point of contact and you really establish a good relationship with these engineers, they can help you with anything you need to ensure things go smoothly and they respond very quickly. Overall, we're very happy with the service from ShipMonk. With any 3PL you will run into issues here and there, it's a complicated logistical system shipping millions of packages a year, but with ShipMonk, together we've ironed out the initial issues and have been smooth sailing to success!

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipMonk customer leaves a glowing 5-star review praising their 3PL service, software, and support team while acknowledging that occasional logistics issues are inevitable.

content.body: The software glitched and charged me over $125 without ever providing a shipping label. I've been trying since January (5 months) to get this resolved, and they keep stringing me along, claiming they're 'working on this situation and investigating further'. It doesn't take 5 months to refund an overcharge, and they can figure out the glitch on their own time. That one overcharge more than cancelled out any savings provided by using the service. Now I have to go to the CC company and see about getting those charges reversed. Not worth it at all.

classification.summary: Customer experienced a $125 billing glitch with no shipping label delivery and unresolved complaint over 5 months, prompting them to seek chargeback and switch providers.

content.body: Look somewhere else! Average 2-3 days just to pick and pack product, their shipping partner is super slow and will lose 1/50 packages, went from 4-5 day delivery average doing it in house with usps and now with shipbob 10-20 day. Shipping times are huge with e-commerce! Implementation process was a mess also.

content.body: The Easyship app and the company really make it easy to sync our orders with our 3PL provider and prepare shipments with our support team. It saves our team a lot of time with fulfilling orders and automatically calculates shipping rates for our customers during checkout. Our new account manager Melody has done a great job of proactively reaching out, checking in and helping solve outstanding issues. Where she could not solve, she would involve the right people on her team to look into the issue. We have had a great experience with Melody and Easyship team!

classification.summary: Satisfied customer praising Easyship's order syncing, shipping rate automation, and account manager support for their 3PL fulfillment operations.

content.body: WHAT WERE THEY THINKING WITH THE NEW LAYOUT. I HAVE BEEN TELLING THEM FOR MONTHS NOT TO SWITCH.. NOW WE ARE LOOKING FOR A NEW SOFTWARE. WHAT A SHAME.

content.body: Avoid ShipBob at all Costs. 1) We shipped to 2 of their warehouses, they lost all of the inventory in 1 of the warehouses and then took THREE months to find it. 2) They say they can ship in your custom shipping box. They can't. No matter what they say they can't. They mailed our customers our product in a generic box and inside that generic box they put our custom box unfolded... as if the customer had ordered an empty box. 3) Their customer service is absolutely unable to help with anything -- all of these errors they create are your problem to resolve. ShipBob customer service will tell you they are "working on it" or "sent it to the team that can resolve it". Nothing will happen and this will / has been going on for FOUR months. Literally. An entire season. As a small business owner time is everything. And ShipBob seems to have no respect for that and worse yet they are unable to do the very thing they advertise: fulfill your orders. Go somewhere else and hire literally any other fulfillment service and save yourself months of headache and frustration.

classification.summary: Small business owner actively seeking to switch from ShipBob due to lost inventory, inability to fulfill custom packaging requirements, and unresponsive customer service over a 4-month period.

content.body: I honestly have nothing good to say about this app. I downloaded the free trial in 2021 and was BOMBARDED by customer support emails and phone calls. They were calling me up to 5 times per day and emailing me to help me set up because I had not printed a label. I ended up deleting the app because I was so annoyed by their calls. I decided to give it another shot today as our business needs a more streamlined shipping solution, but none of our current orders will import from Shopify. content.body: Shipmonk has stupid high shipping. I have products that cost up to $100 to ship and they split it into two shipments AND charge the fulfillment fee for each. Which ends up being an additional $9. You will be paying the fulfillment fee monthly whether you ship the items or not. These fees are the same price as the storage fees so storage is double the price they say it is. Another thing about storage is that the allowable height of a pallet is on the lower side meaning they charge more than you can find elsewhere per pallet. When I started with them, they would reply to my emails and tickets within 24 hrs and now it takes them 4+ days. Many of the questions I ask them are for customers of mine (sometimes shipping delays and even not receiving). And then I often end up just giving my customers their money back While I wait for Shipmonk's reply. I found a company that has cheaper storage (larger pallets for same monthly fee) plus they don't charge the additional fulfillment fees AND they don't have a minimum monthly order. (The storage is literally half the price) And a HUGE advantage of this new company is the amazingly lower shipping cost. This $100 Shipping cost plus $9 fulfillment fees through Shipmonk is now only costing me a total of $30 with this new company - that includes fulfillment! (Went from $109 to $30) I unfortunately still have products in their warehouse and being charged a stupid amount. I have paid this other fulfillment company to pick them up. I will still need to pay Shipmonk a large amount on top of that for this other company to pick them up. Do not go with Shipmonk!!!

classification.summary: Ecommerce brand actively switching from ShipMonk due to excessive shipping costs, hidden fulfillment fees, poor customer service response times, and high storage rates, having found a competitor offering 70% lower total fulfillment costs.

content.body: We've been using Easyship for a couple of years and rely on it daily to ship out our orders. It is very simple to set up and use and the support team is excellent. They are also constantly adding new features and improving the platform which is great to see.

content.body: Updated 2022: Since many flights were cancelled so I would like to give this app second chance for international shipping. But you know what? After two years, their customer service is still suck!!! I was not able to receive handover note emails suddenly so I asked for help, but no one can solve my problem and suddenly the customer service disappeared!!!! I have tried to resend emails to ask for the progress but no one replied. So I gave up and after a month, yes a whole month!!! Suddenly one of your customer service asked me if my problem was solved. That's so funny!! Then I replied why they replied my email after a month since no one solved my problem, CS disappeared again!! Old review:Very BAD customer service. It took a week to reply my question, no one can help if you have problem with your shipping. Even they replied, they didn't answer your question directly. BAD!

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with poor and unresponsive customer service for international shipping, experiencing delays in problem resolution and communication.

content.body: I'm done with this app. I've tried to resolve this several times with Shipstation support, but they tell me it's "working as intended." Here are my issues...1) When I change an order's address in Shopify, the change in address doesn't import into shipstation. I've sent items to the wrong address many times because of this.2) When updating the address in the Shipstation app, the web version doesn't reflect the change, causing orders to be shipped to the wrong address.3) The sync is extremely inaccurate to what is shown in Shopify when shipped via multiple vendors. For some reason, the developers can't figure out how to have an order that shows fulfilled in Shopify to reflect correctly in Shipstation, causing me to spend hours cross-referencing my open orders in Shopify to manually mark them as fulfilled in Shipstation.4) My Etsy orders aren't syncing correctly, causing missed orders. For some reason, Shipstation only imports some of the orders, not all of them from Etsy. Again, I've tried to bring attention to these issues but the Shipstation team shows no interest in resolving them. Instead, they want to treat me like I'm ignorant. It would also be helpful to have a phone number so I could discuss the specific issues live.I'm moving on.

classification.summary: Ecommerce brand actively leaving ShipStation due to critical syncing failures between Shopify/Etsy and their fulfillment system that has caused multiple shipments to wrong addresses.

content.body: This review is primarily for the ShipHero 3PL software. Before moving to ShipHero, we were fulfilling 5K+ orders/mo very manually—no SKUs or inventory tracking, manual 3PL billing for other brands we fulfill for etc. We evaluated several 3PL solutions to help us scale fulfillment our own brands, as well as build out a new warehouse to grow our 3PL company. We finally settled on ShipHero because of the full suite 3PL software they've built rather than using separate apps for what we needed. We scored with Brett Oliveira initially, and then Mike Azimi as our onboarding specialist, and he was AMAZING to work with. The onboarding was super smooth, and we're now shipping 7K+ orders/mo for several brands from our first warehouse running ShipHero within a matter of weeks. Some specific things we really appreciate about ShipHero 3PL software: - Kits! We can create a kit SKU and assign multiple items to be picked/tracked as components in different locations in the warehouse.- The efficiency of the picking flow through the warehouse.- Replenishment reports for optimizing our team to restock pickable locations.- Using Purchase Orders for receiving inventory is super streamlined, and makes it easy for our team to be efficient.- The whole iOS/scanner implementation makes it easy to onboard new employees and make them productive immediately.- Custom orders statuses that we can use to have pickers focus on specific 3PL brands.- Order automation rules for adding SKUs to orders based on the product SKUs (e.g. adding ring box SKUs to ring orders, necklace pouches to necklace orders, etc.)- 3PL billing! We haven’t even scratched the surface yet of all the detailed levels of billing you can apply to your 3PL customers - per pick, n+ picks, bins used in the warehouse, cubic storage used, etc. GREAT STUFF!- There’s bound to be more things here as we explore the software further. The big selling point is that they use their own software in their own warehouses which means they dogwood their own features before providing them to us 3PL software customers. Before we started with ShipHero we visited their Allentown warehouse and got a ton of ideas for how to implement our warehouse layout and workflows. We still have yet to nerd out on Xbox Kinect for our packing stations, but they even have a KB article when we have some time :) We’d definitely recommend ShipHero if you’re looking to scale your 3PL company and want a SAAS solution from a growing company that is continually building features that help you grow with them.

classification.summary: A 3PL company successfully scaled from 5K to 7K+ orders/month using ShipHero software and is sharing positive experiences with their fulfillment and billing capabilities.

content.body: Shipstation is a fantastic platform for managing shipments! I've been using it for over a year and a half, and it continues to surpass my expectations. Pros: - The interface is nicely laid out to give access to all of the tools we need without being overwhelming, with good navigation and tab organization - You can set up customized filter and column views, and save them as tabs for easy repeat reference - The search function with advanced search is very accurate and reliable, with options to fine tune the results extensively - Product info can be updated with selective imports so you're not overwriting any existing data - There are multiple stackable options for product and order organization to give very granular control over your systems - The automation rules are extensive, with virtually every product and order variable usable as an automation trigger - Built-in batch fulfillment with the ability to generate batches through both automation and manual filtering: if you've set the item weights/boxes/customs info/batches/filtering/etc up correctly, you can process orders near-instantly - Networked printer integration: if you set up the connection with Shipstation Connect (free desktop application) on one computer, you can print from Shipstation on any device - Support is quick to respond and very knowledgeable - The documentation is extensive, easy to understand, and simple to navigate through - The pricing is also very affordable for the amount of time we save - It has integrations for all the platforms we use and can manage multiple stores and couriers The only notable con is that Shipstation currently lacks built-in tools for tracking packing materials: you can create custom box sizes and add them to orders seamlessly with both automatic and manual options, but you can't add a weight to a box listing and have the order weight adjust automatically when you change the box, or remove a box from inventory when you process a shipment. Small thing, but annoying: we end up adding the box weight to the product weights so we can batch fulfil our 1-2 item orders, but it messes all the calculations up for multi-item orders. Overall highly recommend, Shipstation is an excellent platform: I've recommended it to several small business owners in passing, and I'd recommend it in general to anyone who has to track orders.

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipStation user with 1.5+ years of experience provides detailed praise for the platform's interface, automation, and support, while noting a minor operational gap around packing material tracking.

content.body: Having used a number of 3PL's over the years we know how hard it is to get a strong 3PL partner. We've been using Shipbob for the last 12 months and they have been a great partner for us. Their level of service and speed at which they get orders out to customers is fantastic (particularly in pandemic times where other 3PL's are struggling to get orders out). Their customer service to merchants is also exceptional. Our account manager Chetan is fantastic, as are Karina and Mahmood in customer service. The only opportunities for us are around streamlining of some administrative processes, which are being addressed by their product team (they're always happy to take onboard any challenge we have and get the product team involved) and price of storage is higher than other 3PL's (but this is because they want quick turns on inventory and don't want it sitting around). Overall, we're very pleased with our partnership with Shipbob.

content.body: I was really excited to work with Shipmonk, their service seemed really great. However after 1 year with them I'm canceling my contract because of the constant loss of inventory that has occurred. Pros: Easy to use integrated software that syncs with Shopify and also automatically downloads my Etsy orders. Inventory tracking feature that updated my Shopify stock. When everything went well, being a Shipmonk customer was great. Orders were automatically fulfilled and it saved hours of work. Cons: They lost thousands of dollars of inventory over the course of a year, a fraction of which was reimbursed. When you sign up for their service the terms and conditions say that even though they might be at fault for lost inventory, they are not financially responsible. It was incredibly frustrating having to email their customer service every week to report an inventory discrepancy. I sell unique clothing designs that have a high production cost so I suspect staff members were stealing my products each time an order was fulfilled. Their Happiness Engineers were always very apologetic each time I reported a claim, but failed to solve the issue or explain why my inventory continually went missing in their warehouses. The rare times that Shipmonk did agree to reimburse me, they only gave me a $10 credit per unit which was a small percentage of the production cost. The lost inventory has negatively impacted my business' reputation. Because of Shipmonk's errors, each month I had to message customers to apologize that their orders could no longer be fulfilled. Many of the missing products were irreplaceable. Their software syncing system would list products as in stock on my website, then suddenly change to an inventory level of 0 when the order was being fulfilled, changing the order status to backordered. Because of Shipmonk's negligence, I've lost thousands of dollars worth of sales plus thousands in lost inventory.

classification.summary: Customer is actively canceling ShipMonk after experiencing significant inventory losses, poor reimbursement policies, and software sync issues that damaged their business reputation and sales.

content.body: Scale your ecommerce business with Shipstation. Don't waste time manually creating labels and sending tracking info to your customers. content.body: I am writing this review as a last ditch attempt to get support after over 25 chats and emails dating back at least a year. From the start I have been using the print automation to print an Endicia Pay Upon Use Return Label when I print a shipping label. About a year ago the automation got a bug, and will randomly stop working. Once it stops, every order there after will not print the return label. I have over 25 chats and emails and was told my case is "escalated", but since it is not a wide spread issue there is no timeline when it will be fixed. The work around was to use the shipstation branded return portal. OK, I set it up. Now this is a work around, but it costs me more money. This is because I can only use 1 type of return. So if a package goes out USPS first class mail for $5, it has to come back using Priority Mail for $8. Not great, but hopefully it would get me by until the automation was fixed. About 2 months ago the returns portal stopped working. It now tells my customers their order has not yet shipped and no label can be produced. This case was also "escalated" but there is no date for it to be fixed because it is not a wide spread issue...so I am told. Now I spend way too much time dealing with returns because both options I have to automate this process are busted and I cant get anyone to fix one or the other. I get emails and calls from people needing return labels that I have to stop what I am doing and go manually produce the label. If these issues cannot be fixed in the near future I will be leaving shipstation for a competitor. I like everything else about shipstation, and when everything functions it meets my needs perfectly. But the support has been terrible, I keep getting the same scripted lines over and over, and my issues are no closer to being fixed than they were a year ago. Your move shipstation, your move.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation user is actively considering switching to a competitor due to unresolved bugs in return label automation and poor support responsiveness over the past year.

content.body: Great app integation to simplify the fulfilment process. Plenty of direct integrations to many of the major shipping providers here in Australia and also a great API if you need to integrate with something a bit more custom. Support is excellent and very responsive.

classification.summary: Customer in Australia praising their current fulfillment solution's app integration, shipping provider connections, API customization, and support quality.

content.body: Pro: -Generally positive experience for orders placed and fully shipped within a few days. For orders with longer wait times, or orders that are split into multiple shipments we have some issues. -Automated completion of customs forms for international shipments. Con: -Long times between automated store synchronization: Can be over two hours between store updates from Shopify. There is the option to manually update the stores, but it is not feasible to manually update the store 24/7 if there are other processes that rely on timely information. -Poor support for split-shipped orders. If one item from an order ships, Shipstation will not process any edits to the order (e.g. an item added to the order, an item removed from the order, other quantity adjustments to the order, address edits or updates) -Orders only open to edits for a short time: After 60 days no order edits or changes are passed to Shipstation. With supply chain delays we regularly have orders that are older than 60 days that require changes or edits. -Long loading times in Shipstation: with orders that have a large number of items, or orders that have one or more previous shipments there is a VERY LONG and noticeable delay from the time an order is opened in Shipstation before the page fully loads and we are able to make adjustments in weight, shipping service, return labels, or print a label. -Unreliable USPS tracking numbers generated during peak seasons: Recently, large quantities of tracking numbers that are generated for our USPS shipments have not been reset before being reused. These tracking numbers provide the shipment history of shipments that were completed previously during the year, and we have been unable to find a solution to reset those so that our customers receive accurate tracking information for their order.

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with Shipstation's slow synchronization, poor split-shipment handling, order edit limitations, slow loading times, and unreliable USPS tracking generation during peak seasons.

content.body: SHIPBOB Australia B2C Fulfilment ex Altona Warehouse - Absolute HORROR!We used shipbob for just under 6 months via WIX and Shopify Store. Originally attracted by what appears a solid and streamlined Tech Platform with experienced fulfilment providers for reasonable and transparent cost.Having experienced shipbob for months we offer our strong warning to store operators. Every Warehouse receiving order was either lost for 7 days and took a further 7-14 days until the stock was listed in inventory for dispatch. On one occasion the warehouse told the delivery contractor they will not accept anything via Shipbob which was the indication for us to make an urgent move.As to the actual picking and shipping of our orders their service appeared efficient and good (but our customers did not take kindly to excessive delays with receiving of good into the FC). We had countless email communication with the support team who seem to be the go-between melbourne warehouse and US operations team. Once they ran out of excuses they just started to ignore us which made it a nightmare for us as we had no information to give our customers. In general terms we would say Shipbob will be unlikely to survive in Australia and they present a real danger to any business that makes the mistake top rely on them. And in the end we lost many orders because of them and scored the anger of many customers whilst Shipbob did not feel the need to compensate us even with a dollar.

classification.summary: Australian ecommerce business is warning others about ShipBob's poor warehouse receiving processes and unresponsive support that caused lost orders and customer dissatisfaction.
